---
subject: "What I’ve been reading"
from: "Marginal REVOLUTION <donotreply@wordpress.com>"
to: ""
date: 2025-08-25 05:03:06
labels: ["CATEGORY_PERSONAL", "INBOX", "Tyler Cowen", "UNREAD"]
label_ids: ["CATEGORY_PERSONAL", "INBOX", "Label_2466199532210586699", "UNREAD"]
---
David Woodman, [The First King of England: Aethelstan and the Birth of a Kingdom](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=01bbc442e222424c322c4596ac24bdcacee5a511e414e597ed635073d86e3151&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91305&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=56c9a61c132947839e5a3df0765432fa&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=dfbef3af868cd2174d91a5868e5ec2e2). An excellent work. One of the best books on early English history, and also one of the best books on how the Dark Ages morphed into early Medieval times. Usually I find treatments in both areas difficult to follow, but this one produces a coherent and also non-exaggerated narrative. It also will make you want to visit Northumbria.
Edmund Phelps, [My Journeys in Economic Theory](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=c240eb1824f8eaccaee00ca4fbabdc1f9de51c48c02666a9b6756bf8ecc32892&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91305&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=0bed0b0617f9fce5390330d5e3a4ad21&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=dfbef3af868cd2174d91a5868e5ec2e2). A fascinating memoir, I had not known he was so obsessed with Rawls and Nagel. He also loved the tenor Franco Corelli, and was a Birgit Nilsson fan too. Recommended, for those who like this sort of thing, and who already are familiar with the cast of characters.
Bench Ansfield, [Born in Flames: The Business of Arson and the Remaking of the American City](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=55f470398dea7ba2bfb2f38c2109b04b0bc3cebb5af0684de7af1ab947f791ec&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91305&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=047036e67a8131875d98cc17b4f33883&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=dfbef3af868cd2174d91a5868e5ec2e2). Whenever a book demonstrates what people in New Jersey have known for decades, usually it is a good book.
Andrew Sean Greer, [Less: A Novel](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=4971b21ed4bc69b34fa0ec8bcff1ca62ae2b220219bb752793557983fe629c7e&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91305&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=3e89c5e94d92aa9f88e3cb89998c5c4d&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=dfbef3af868cd2174d91a5868e5ec2e2). I do not like much in contemporary American fiction, but so far I am quite enjoying this one.
Bernd Roeck, [The World at First Light: A New History of the Renaissance](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=ffe006d79ebdbfeaff907c7584a3dc3bdf0f0687df69a2028646b6c9062363c5&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91305&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=e30419bc35f1da75e865b1526c0f728c&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=dfbef3af868cd2174d91a5868e5ec2e2). 934 pp. of text, covers too many topics in too desultory a fashion?
Pablo A. Pena, [Human Capital for Humans: An Accessible Introduction to the Economic Science of the People](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=50581208a54b7770f6ea0ee3243057b10ba26f9d193359137e573ad0bc1a04d9&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91305&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=ac454ba943048d0dd8ba48a4b8ff68d4&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=dfbef3af868cd2174d91a5868e5ec2e2), is a good popular-level introduction to human capital theory.
There is Carl Benedikt Frey, [How Progress Ends: Technology, Innovation, and the Fate of Nations](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=04d119702ad0972362324fde6f0cec3475e2a116e74ef8d235b62432b69aa6c1&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91305&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=20e93b80eda67b7c5a6fe96156e1d124&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=dfbef3af868cd2174d91a5868e5ec2e2).
